name: general_issue_retrieval_set_2026-04-26
description: General issue-lookup retrieval probes for paralegal research workflows. These prompts intentionally omit case names and citations. The goal is to retrieve relevant source documents and chunks for human review, not to force fully verified case-specific answers.

evaluation_goal.retrieval: Expected relevant cases should appear in the top retrieved chunks, preferably top 8.
evaluation_goal.generation: Answers should frame results as exploratory research leads unless the retrieved chunks directly support the proposition.
evaluation_goal.verification: Claims based on broad issue matching should be marked supported only when the retrieved text directly supports them; otherwise they should be possibly_supported or unsupported.

recommended_metrics.case_recall_at_8: At least one expected_relevant_cases entry appears in the first 8 retrieved chunks.
recommended_metrics.case_precision_at_8: At least half of the distinct case names in the first 8 chunks are listed in expected_relevant_cases or acceptable_related_cases.
recommended_metrics.answer_safety: The answer identifies retrieved cases as research leads and avoids stating a holding unless the context directly supports it.
recommended_metrics.verification_ceiling: Exploratory or analogical statements should not receive a stronger label than possibly_supported unless tied to direct evidence.

items.query: What Supreme Court cases discuss race-conscious university admissions and equal protection?
expected_relevant_cases: Students for Fair Admissions, Inc. v. President and Fellows of Harvard College
items.current_sparse_retrieval: Strong top-8 hit on expected case.
